cards.name: Alexander the Great

abilities.name: Kataktas
abilities.text: Your attacks deal +1 damage against warriors with more life than you.

cards.flavor: Driven to surpass his father, Alexander's ambition extends to the ends of the world.
cards.background:
Regarded by many as the greatest conqueror in history, Alexander III of Macedon created an empire spanning three continents. As leader, Alexander always fought on the front lines with his men, inspiring enormous loyalty.

Armored in a lightweight but effective linen cuirass and mounted on Bucephalus, his beloved horse, Alexander developed tactics which emphasized the use of sarrissae, pikes that measure over six and a half meter in length. With his victories portended by the Oracle of Delphi, Alexander is an unstoppable force in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Kataktas: Your attacks deal +1 damage against warriors with more life than you.

cards.name: Oracle of Delphi

abilities.name: Propheteia
abilities.text: Look at all face-down cards. You may trade the placement of two of your face-down cards.

cards.flavor: At his temple in Delphi, Apollo speaks of the fate of the world through his Oracle, Pythia.
cards.raw_source_text: Propheteia - Reveal: Look at all face-down cards. You may trade the placement of two of your face-down cards.

cards.name: Sarissae

abilities.name: Phalanx
abilities.text: Once per turn, when a warrior moves into a space on this weapon's attack grid, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.flavor: The success of the Macedonian phalanx rests with these 6.3-meter pikes.
cards.raw_source_text: Phalanx: Once per turn, when a warrior moves into a space on this weapon's attack grid, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.name: Linen Cuirass

abilities.name: Linothorax
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have lower life than the attacking warrior.

cards.flavor: Glued linen forms the shell of this lightweight breastplate.
cards.raw_source_text: Linothorax: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have lower life than the attacking warrior.

cards.name: Bucephalus

abilities.name: Strategike Thesis
abilities.text: Once per round, after you make an attack, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: "Ox Head", Alexander the Great's faithful horse, carries him proudly into many battles.
cards.raw_source_text: Strategike Thesis: Once per round, after you make an attack, you may move one space.

cards.name: Leonidas

abilities.name: Molon Lave
abilities.text: Once per round, after you deal damage to an enemy warrior, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: Against overwhelming odds, Spartan Kink Leonidas and his brave 300 fight to the death so that others may live.
cards.background:
Spartan King Leonidas marched against insurmountable odds and traded his own life so that others could live. Wielding the Spartan lakonian and protected by the redoubtable hoplon shield, this bold commander and his 300 bodyguards held the Persian legions long enough to send runners to warn the rest of Greece of the impending invasion.

Watched over by Nemesis, the goddess of retribution, his strength and steadfast discipline are as unbreakable in the Anachronism™ arena as they were in the passes of Thermoplae.
cards.raw_source_text: Molon Lave: Once per round, after you deal damage to an enemy warrior, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Nemesis

abilities.name: Ekdikisi
abilities.text: The next card you reveal gains +10 initiative.

cards.flavor: The goddess of retribution, Nemesis punishes excessive pride and undeserved happiness.
cards.raw_source_text: Ekdikisi: The next card you reveal gains +10 initiative.

cards.name: Spartan Lakonian

abilities.name: Epithetiko Pleonektima
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 and your attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage while your opponent has no weapon.

cards.flavor: When the battle draws to close quarters, this short sword allows the Spartan hoplite to efficiently deal death.
cards.raw_source_text: Epithetiko Pleonektima: Your attack rolls gain +1 and your attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage while your opponent has no weapon.

cards.name: Muscle Cuirass

abilities.name: Tutlela
abilities.text: After you make a roll that is not an attack roll, the result is considered doubles. Discard this card after use.

cards.flavor: Weighing up to 3.3 kilograms, this sculpted bronze breastplate provides excellent protection.
cards.raw_source_text: Tutlela: After you make a roll that is not an attack roll, the result is considered doubles. Discard this card after use.

cards.name: Hoplon

abilities.name: I tan I epi tas
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have a face-up weapon card.

cards.flavor: These round, reinforces shields are the best protection wielded by the Greek Hoplites.
cards.raw_source_text: I tan I epi tas: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have a face-up weapon card.

cards.name: Herakles

abilities.name: Kairdoskopos
abilities.text: Once per round, after an enemy warrior moves into your basic attack grid, you may immediately make a basic attack against that warrior.

cards.flavor: Herakles' boundless strength and courage eventually earn him a place among the gods on Mount Olympus.
cards.background:
Reputed as the son of Zeus, Herakles was driven into a fit of madness during which he killed his family. To atone, he undertook the Twelve Labors for which he is famous. According to legend, having lived a noble life of courage and repentance, Herakles was raised to Mount Olympus as a demigod.

Weilding the kopis and boasting the legendary belt of Hippolyte, Herakles arrives equipped to take on all comers in the Anachronismtm arena. With Zeus, hi father and rules of the gods of Olympus watching over him, Herakles won't let anyone stand in his way of victroy.
cards.raw_source_text: Kairdoskopos: Once per round, after an enemy warrior moves into your basic attack grid, you may immediately make a basic attack against that warrior.

cards.name: Zeus

abilities.name: Diatagma tou Olimpou
abilities.text: At the beginning of any round, you may name an ability. That ability has no effect that round. After you name an ability, discard this card.

cards.flavor: Zeus rules Mount Olympus and all the Greek gods who dwell there.
cards.raw_source_text: Diatagma tou Olimpou: At the beginning of any round, you may name an ability. That ability has no effect that round. After you name an ability, discard this card.

cards.name: Kopis

abilities.name: Epithesi!
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if you have moved this turn.

cards.flavor: Curved blades like the kopis make excellent slashing weapons.
cards.raw_source_text: Epithesi!: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if you have moved this turn.

cards.name: Greaves

abilities.name: Exostrakismos
abilities.text: Once per round, you may re-roll one die of one of your defense rolls.

cards.flavor: These lace-able shin guards proctect the wearer against missile and melee blows to the lower legs.
cards.raw_source_text: Exostrakismos: Once per round, you may re-roll one die of one of your defense rolls.

cards.name: Belt of Hippolyte

abilities.name: Zoni Dexiotexnias
abilities.text: You gain +4 experience.

cards.flavor: During Herakles' ninth labor, he is forced to kill Amazonian Queen Hippolyte to take her belt back to Eurytheus.
cards.raw_source_text: Zoni Dexiotexnias: You gain +4 experience.

cards.name: Milo of Croton

abilities.name: Xoris Fragmo
abilities.text: Your base attacks deal +1 damage while you have no face-up weapon cards.

cards.flavor: Milo's amazing strength and indomitable pride help him win six Olympic wrestling tournaments.
cards.background:
The Olympic Games represented the stage on which the greatest athletes of the ancient world proved their skill. Since the earliest days of the games, most competitors were satisfied to wear the victor's wreath just once in their lifetime. Milo of Croton, the greatest wrestler of ancient Greece, won the kotynos, the winner's laurles, six times.

Protected by the impressive liontari - an armor fashieoned from the skin of a lion - and wielding the mighty ropalo club, Milo puts his unbeaten streak to the test. All who enter the Anachronismtm arena will know the strength of the world's greatest ancient Olympic wrestler
cards.raw_source_text: Xoris Fragmo: Your base attacks deal +1 damage while you have no face-up weapon cards.

cards.name: Pythagoras

abilities.name: Sintomotera Odos
abilities.text: Move one space diagonally.

cards.flavor: A philosopher and mathematician, Pythagoras challenges thought with his insight and understanding.
cards.raw_source_text: Sintomotera Odos - Action: Move one space diagonally.

cards.name: Ropalo

abilities.name: Dinato Xtipima
abilities.text: Turn your warrior to face any direction. You may immediately make an attack with this weapon against each warrior in this weapon's attack grid.

cards.flavor: The ropalo, a large club, is used simply to bludgeon opponents into submission.
cards.raw_source_text: Dinato Xtipima - Reveal: Turn your warrior to face any direction. You may immediately make an attack with this weapon against each warrior in this weapon's attack grid.

cards.name: Liontari

abilities.name: Apotropi
abilities.text: When you roll doubles on a defense roll, the attack misses.

cards.flavor: The spirit of the Lion courses through the body of whomever wears its skin.
cards.raw_source_text: Apotropi: When you roll doubles on a defense roll, the attack misses.

cards.name: Kotynos

abilities.name: O Draskelismos Tou Protathliti
abilities.text: Once per round, you may re-roll your attack roll.

cards.flavor: The olive wreath symbolizes victory for the fastest, bravest and strongest.
cards.raw_source_text: O Draskelismos Tou Protathliti: Once per round, you may re-roll your attack roll.

cards.name: Oda Nobunaga

abilities.name: Roukou
abilities.text: You win ties with warriors that do not have the Roukou ability, regardless of experience.

cards.flavor: Nobunaga's battlefield brillance has secured his place as one of the most brutally effective military leaders of all time.
cards.background:
One of the most cunning and intelligent tacticians the world has ever known, Oda Nobunaga attempted to unify Japan under one sword. A military innovator of the highest order, Nobunaga's enemies were rarely prepared for his stratagems.

Oda was renowned for the masterful deployment of his archers. Trained in kyudo, the Japanese way of the bow, he rains death from a distance with his yumi, the ancient Japanese longbow. Oda's battle acumen will be tested to the fullest by his opposition in the Anachronismtm arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Roukou: You win ties with warriors that do not have the Roukou ability, regardless of experience.

cards.name: Izanagi

abilities.name: Zonrei
abilities.text: Roll two dice. If that total is less than your experience, gain 2 life.

cards.flavor: Izanagi, god of the life and all that is heavenly, creates the Japanese islands with his sister and wife, Izanami.
cards.raw_source_text: Zonrei - Reveal: Roll two dice. If that total is less than your experience, gain 2 life.

cards.name: Yumi

abilities.name: Zanshin
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage this round.

cards.flavor: The Japanese bow is unique in every sense. It is asymmetrical, over 2 meters in length and fatally accurate.
cards.raw_source_text: Zanshin - Reveal: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage this round.

cards.name: Do

abilities.name: Yae
abilities.text: Once per turn, you may re-roll your defense roll.

cards.flavor: Eleven to thirteen overlapping plates make the do a very effective and flexible piece of body armor.
cards.raw_source_text: Yae: Once per turn, you may re-roll your defense roll.

cards.name: Kyudo

abilities.name: Shuuchuu
abilities.text: Your attack rolls with ranged weapons gain +1.

cards.flavor: Japanese archery, known as kyudo or "the war of the bow", uses Zen meditation as part of the technique.
cards.raw_source_text: Shuuchuu: Your attack rolls with ranged weapons gain +1.

cards.name: Nakano Takeko

abilities.name: Roshui-gun
abilities.text: After you hit a warrior with a weapon attack, you may move 1 space.

cards.flavor: As head of the roshui-gun, weapon master Nakano Takeko leads her female samurai into battle.
cards.background:
Nakano Takeko was a master of the naginata, the traditional weapon of the female samurai. She fought for the Aizu clan in support of the shogunate in the final days of the Meiji Restoration. When Wakamatsu castle came under attack, Nakano Takeko fought in its defense, cutting down many enemy troops before meeting her end.

To assume the role of Nakano Takeko is to uphold the firm belief in martial arts and military training that served her throughout her lifetime. This mindset separates the common soldier form the greatest warriors in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Roshui-gun: After you hit a warrior with a weapon attack, you may move 1 space.

cards.name: Bokken

abilities.name: Precision
abilities.text: Once per turn, you may re-roll one die on an attack roll.

abilities.name: Pair
abilities.text: You may use two "Bokken" cards in this game.

cards.flavor: Victory with a wooden sword demonstrates superior confidence and domination over bladed opponents.
cards.raw_source_text:
Precision: Once per turn, you may re-roll one die on an attack roll.
Pair: You may use two "Bokken" cards in this game.

cards.name: Amaterasu

abilities.name: Hikarimono
abilities.text: At the beginning of this round, if you have less life than all other warriors, you gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.flavor: Amaterasu rules the Shinto religion as surpreme goddess of the sun and queen of the kami.
cards.raw_source_text: Hikarimono - Reveal: At the beginning of this round, if you have less life than all other warriors, you gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.name: Gorin no Sho

abilities.name: Heiho
abilities.text: Once per turn, when you make an attack roll, you may reduce the value of one of your dice by 1. If the dice becomes doubles, this may result in a critical hit.

cards.flavor: The Book of Five Elements represents Musashi's thoughts on how to win in every possible situation.
cards.raw_source_text: Heiho: Once per turn, when you make an attack roll, you may reduce the value of one of your dice by 1. If the dice becomes doubles, this may result in a critical hit.

cards.name: Naginata

abilities.name: Sune-Uchi
abilities.text: Choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If the total is more than your opponent's experience, the opposing warrior gets -2 initiative for the next round.

cards.flavor: Sweeping movements make this curved blade, mounted on the end of a long, wooden pole, devastating against both infantry and cavalry.
cards.raw_source_text: Sune-Uchi - Reveal: Choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If the total is more than your opponent's experience, the opposing warrior gets -2 initiative for the next round.

cards.name: Mempo

abilities.name: Ojikedzuku
abilities.text: Once per turn, choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If that total is greater than or equal to their experience, you may move that warrior up to 2 spaces and change their facing.

cards.flavor: Samurai use these terrifying armored facemasks to shake the will of their enemies.
cards.raw_source_text: Ojikedzuku - Action: Once per turn, choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If that total is greater than or equal to their experience, you may move that warrior up to 2 spaces and change their facing.

cards.name: Kemuri-Kona

abilities.name: Enmaku
abilities.text: You may move to any space in your starting row.

cards.flavor: These smoke grenades produce clouds of noxious, burning gas and make for very effective distractions.
cards.raw_source_text: Enmaku - Reveal: You may move to any space in your starting row.

cards.name: Saito Musashibo Benkei

abilities.name: Toushimanman
abilities.text: After you hit an opponent with a basic attack, they cannot attack you with a weapon this round.

cards.flavor: A giant of a man, with legendary strength and skill, warrior monk Benkei of the Yamabushi comes to embody some of the greatest qualities of the Japanese warrior.
cards.background:
Saito Musashibo Benki tested his prowess in combat at every chance. Infamous for challenging warriors at river crossings, Benkei collected countless swords from his defeated opponents.

Benkei wields the supreme weapon of his day, the masterfully crafted katana. Bishamon-ten, the Japanese god of war and happiness, watches over Benkei's tireless loyalty and spirit. Astride the Japanese war-horse, Saito Musashibo Benki stands against any opponent who dares enter the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Toushimanman: After you hit an opponent with a basic attack, they cannot attack you with a weapon this round.

cards.name: Bishamon-ten

abilities.name: Hogosha
abilities.text: All warriors' attack rolls gain +1.

cards.flavor: Shinto god of war and happiness, as well as one of the seven kami of luck, Bishamon protects the cardinal north and the Japanese people from danger.
cards.raw_source_text: Hogosha: All warriors' attack rolls gain +1.

cards.name: Katana

abilities.name: Ki ken tai itchi
abilities.text: When you hit with this weapon, the attacker and defender each roll one die and add his or her warrior's experience. If your total is higher, you deal +1 damage this attack.

cards.flavor: The katana represents the heart and soul of the samurai; together, they cut down all who stand in the way of its curved blade.
cards.raw_source_text: Ki ken tai itchi: When you hit with this weapon, the attacker and defender each roll one die and add his or her warrior's experience. If your total is higher, you deal +1 damage this attack.

cards.name: Kimono

abilities.name: Unrestricted
abilities.text: Once per turn, after an opponent attacks you and misses, roll two dice. If that total is less than your experience, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: The nearly weightless kimono robe allows swift movement and is a great place to conceal weapons.
cards.raw_source_text: Unrestricted: Once per turn, after an opponent attacks you and misses, roll two dice. If that total is less than your experience, you may move one space.

cards.name: Haidate

abilities.name: Shinogeru
abilities.text: When you are attacked, your defense roll gains +1 if you have more experience than the attacker.

cards.flavor: Thigh plates protect the upper legs with reinforced strips of leather.
cards.raw_source_text: Shinogeru: When you are attacked, your defense roll gains +1 if you have more experience than the attacker.

cards.name: Uma

abilities.name: Subayai
abilities.text: Once per round, move two spaces.

cards.flavor: Native Japanese horses stand stout and short, allowing them to maneuver with great agility.
cards.raw_source_text: Subayai - Action: Once per round, move two spaces.

cards.name: Miyamoto Musashi

abilities.name: Niten Ichi Ryu
abilities.text: Your next attack this turn deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: After killing his first opponent at the age of 13, Musashi remains undefeated through sixty-one duels.
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Niten Ichi Ryu - Action: Your next attack this turn deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Musha-Shuyo

abilities.name: Sho Shin
abilities.text: For this round, all hits for all players are critical hits.

cards.flavor: Surviving the warrior pilgimage signifies the birth of a true samurai.
cards.raw_source_text: Sho Shin - Reveal: For this round, all hits for all players are critical hits.

cards.name: Ninja

abilities.name: Ninjutsu
abilities.text: Your attacks deal +1 damage while you are not in the defending opponent's basic attack grid.

abilities.name: Shinobi
abilities.text: This warrior may not use two-handed weapons.

cards.flavor: Ninja assassins, "those who move in secrecy", travel through the shadows towards their mark.
cards.background:
Shadowed, swift and sure, the Ninja were the perfectly honed assasins of ancient Japan. Renowned for their ability to reach the most inaccessible targets, their legend lives on to this day in countless references and whispered anecdotes. Write your own legends commanding one of the ninja in battles against warriors in the Anachronism arena.

The ninja-to and shuriken are at your disposal as the Ninja sends victims to the embrace of Izanami, the Japanese god of death. Using stealth, cunning and secrecy, the Ninja stalks and eliminates targets in any arena.
cards.raw_source_text:
Ninjutsu: Your attacks deal +1 damage while you are not in the defending opponent's basic attack grid.
Shinobi: This warrior may not use two-handed weapons.

cards.name: Izanami

abilities.name: Ma
abilities.text: Roll 2 dice. If the total is greater than 7 (or greater than 5 if you are a wind warrior), then you may move to any empty space in the arena.

cards.flavor: Izanami, sister and wife of Izanagi, rules the underworld as the goddess of death and all things dark.
cards.raw_source_text: Ma - Reveal: Roll 2 dice. If the total is greater than 7 (or greater than 5 if you are a wind warrior), then you may move to any empty space in the arena.

cards.name: Ninja-to

abilities.name: Tamokuteki
abilities.text: When making attack rolls with this weapon, roll three dice and choose which two to use.

cards.flavor: Many samurai meet their end at the short, straight blade of this weapon.
cards.raw_source_text: Tamokuteki: When making attack rolls with this weapon, roll three dice and choose which two to use.

cards.name: Shinobo Shozoku

abilities.name: Kosokoso
abilities.text: Attack rolls against you that are doubles do not count as critical hits.

cards.flavor: As black as midnight, the loose fitting pants and overlapping layered jacket of this outfit allow the ninja to blend into the shadows while concealing a host of weapons.
cards.raw_source_text: Kosokoso: Attack rolls against you that are doubles do not count as critical hits.

cards.name: Shuriken

abilities.name: Ningu
abilities.text: Make a basic attack at +0 against any warrior that is not adjacent to your warrior.

cards.flavor: These razor-sharp throwing stars cut through the air with great accuracy, dealing silent death from the shadows.
cards.raw_source_text: Ningu - Action: Make a basic attack at +0 against any warrior that is not adjacent to your warrior.

cards.name: Canute the Great

abilities.name: En
abilities.text: This card counts as all elements.

cards.flavor: Danish King and legendary member of the Jomsvikings, Canute wears three crowns, including the Crown of England.
cards.background:
Known equally for his wisdom as a ruler and his might on the battlefield, Canute the Great was a terrifying combatant in his youth but a fair and just king in later life. As ruler of Denmark, Canute conquered Norway and England, as well as several other smaller lands before converting to Christianity and becoming a respected and benevolent ruler.

Guided by the wisdom of Odin and wielding the fatally efficient langseax, Canute stands undaunted against all opponents. His chain coif, the healsbeorg, provides extra protection in the most testing circumstances. Canute's mixture of wisdom, strength, and battlefield experience is a potent force in the Anachronism area.
cards.raw_source_text: En: This card counts as all elements.

cards.name: Odin

abilities.name: Innsikt
abilities.text: When making attack or defense rolls, only roll one die. Your roll is equal to the die roll plus the attacking or defending opponent's initiative for this round.

cards.flavor: One-eyed Odin leads the Aesir as the supreme god of the Norse pantheon.
cards.raw_source_text: Innsikt: When making attack or defense rolls, only roll one die. Your roll is equal to the die roll plus the attacking or defending opponent's initiative for this round.

cards.name: Langseax

abilities.name: Hurtig
abilities.text: Your attack rolls with this weapon get +1 for each of your other face-up support cards.

cards.flavor: The langseax doubles as a long shaft and short sword for Norse warriors.
cards.raw_source_text: Hurtig: Your attack rolls with this weapon get +1 for each of your other face-up support cards.

cards.name: Healsbeorgs

abilities.name: Beskyttelse
abilities.text: After you are hit by a critical hit, reduce all damage from that attack to 0 and discard this card.

cards.flavor: Interlocking rings woven into the mail coif protect the head and neck.
cards.raw_source_text: Beskyttelse: After you are hit by a critical hit, reduce all damage from that attack to 0 and discard this card.

cards.name: Crown of England

abilities.name: Konge
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 if you are a metal warrior. Your defense rolls get -1 and your attacks deal +1 damage if you are a fire warrior.

cards.flavor: The king wears the crown as a symbol of power and dominance over the English isles.
cards.raw_source_text: Konge: Your attack rolls gain +1 if you are a metal warrior. Your defense rolls get -1 and your attacks deal +1 damage if you are a fire warrior.

cards.name: Harald Hardrada

abilities.name: Hardfør
abilities.text: Once per turn, when you take damage, you may roll two dice. If that total is less than 7, you take 1 less damage, to a minimum of 1.

cards.flavor: A renowned warrior and Norwegian king, Harald is famed for his ability to take castles and for his love of plunder.
cards.background:
The archetypal Viking, Harald Hardrada was one of the most successful and wealthy of the seaborne raiders. A born conqueror, he captured and sacked over eighty castles in Africa alone.

Inspired by the might of Thor, Harald swings his great langox in battle with fatal intent. Protected by a Viking hjelm and calling upon the retributive power of Thor’s golden hammer, Mjollnir, Haralad smashes all who stand in his way in the Anachonism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Hardfør: Once per turn, when you take damage, you may roll two dice. If that total is less than 7, you take 1 less damage, to a minimum of 1.

cards.name: Beowulf

abilities.name: Tenacity
abilities.text: During their turns, opposing warriors must spend an additional action to move out of a space in any of your attack grids.

cards.flavor: Sixth century Geat King Beowulf rides the waves in search of a worthy challenge.
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Tenacity: During their turns, opposing warriors must spend an additional action to move out of a space in any of your attack grids.

cards.name: Thor

abilities.name: Torden
abilities.text: After you roll doubles on a defense roll, deal 1 damage to the attacking warrior.

cards.flavor: Thor, the Norse god of thunder, protects human and god alike from the forces of evil.
cards.raw_source_text: Torden: After you roll doubles on a defense roll, deal 1 damage to the attacking warrior.

cards.name: Byrnies

abilities.name: Stalwart
abilities.text: Add +2 to your defensive rolls for this round.

cards.flavor: Thousands of interlocking rings make this chain mail count worth its weight in protection.
cards.raw_source_text: Stalwart - Reveal: Add +2 to your defensive rolls for this round.

cards.name: Langox

abilities.name: Tung
abilities.text: Your other cards get -2 initiative.

cards.flavor: A lethal offensive choice, the langox leaves its wielder vulnerable on dense.
cards.raw_source_text: Tung: Your other cards get -2 initiative.

cards.name: Hjelm

abilities.name: Beskyttelsebriller
abilities.text: When you roll two dice for anything other than attack or defense, you may choose to add or subtract 2 from the roll.

cards.flavor: The Norse helmet is famous for its metal "goggles", which protect the eyes, nose and cheekbones.
cards.raw_source_text: Beskyttelsebriller: When you roll two dice for anything other than attack or defense, you may choose to add or subtract 2 from the roll.

cards.name: Mjollnir

abilities.name: Slegge
abilities.text: After you are hit by a basic attack, roll two dice. If that total is greater than the attacker's experience, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.flavor: Mjollnir, Thor's golden hammer, is so powerful that even Thor himself must wear magical iron gauntlets to wield it.
cards.raw_source_text: Slegge: After you are hit by a basic attack, roll two dice. If that total is greater than the attacker's experience, deal 1 damage to that warrior.

cards.name: Freydis Eiriksdottir
cards.flavor: Greenlanders and Icelanders leave their homes in search of Vinland, a land shrouded in myth, yet held to be real. Leading these men and women on the quest for this new land is Freydis, daughter of the infamous Eirik the Red, and her husband Thorward.
cards.background:
Freydis lived up to her father’s expectations. Eirik the Red was on of the greatest explorers of all time and his daughter, Freydis, continued her people’s quest for the legendary Vinland. A mysterious and enigmatic woman, she was renowned for her courage and her fighting prowess.

Protected by her leather armor and the favor of the Norse war god Tyr, Freydis cuts her way across the Anachronism arena with her light and accurate skegox francisca. Freydis deals death to anyone who stands in her way.
cards.raw_source_text: (no card text)

cards.name: Tyr

abilities.name: Mot
abilities.text: After you make a roll, if any of your dice is a 1, the roll is considered doubles, even if the dice are different.

cards.flavor: Boldest of the Norse gods, Tyr inspires heroism and courage in battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Mot: After you make a roll, if any of your dice is a 1, the roll is considered doubles, even if the dice are different.

cards.name: Grendel

abilities.name: Intimidation
abilities.text: Choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If the total is greater than that warrior's experience, that warrior must spend an additional action to attack this round.

cards.flavor: Grendel the giant will dine with anyone who enters "his" hall after dark.
cards.raw_source_text: Intimidation - Reveal: Choose an opposing warrior and roll two dice. If the total is greater than that warrior's experience, that warrior must spend an additional action to attack this round.

cards.name: Skegox Francisca

abilities.name: Kaste
abilities.text: Deal 1 damage to all warriors not adjacent to your warrior.

abilities.name: Liten
abilities.text: You may use another weapon in this game.

cards.flavor: Boldest of the Norse gods, Tyr inspires heroism and courage in battle.
cards.raw_source_text:
Kaste - Reveal: Deal 1 damage to all warriors not adjacent to your warrior.
Liten: You may use another weapon in this game.

cards.name: Leiter

abilities.name: Naturlig
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 if you are a water warrior.

cards.flavor: Leather armor is relatively inexpensive and is the most common of the Norse armors.
cards.raw_source_text: Naturlig: Your defense rolls gain +1 if you are a water warrior.

cards.name: Elhaz Rune

abilities.name: Hellig
abilities.text: Choose a warrior with more life than you. Move to any space in that warrior's attack grid.

cards.flavor: Norse warriors bear the Elhaz rune as a symbol of protection.
cards.raw_source_text: Hellig - Action: Choose a warrior with more life than you. Move to any space in that warrior's attack grid.

cards.name: Grettir the Strong

abilities.name: Sterk
abilities.text: Your attacks with axes deal +1 damage. Your attack rolls that are doubles do not count as critical hits.

cards.flavor: Grettir, outlaw son of Asmund, is the epitome of the warrior poet. However, ill luck stalks him all his life as does a bad reputation.
cards.background:
The greatest figure in the Icelandic sagas, Grettir the Stong was not an archetypal hero. Though possessing enormous strength, great fighting ability and a talent for poetry, Grettir was easily angered and spent much of his life as an outlaw.

Wielding the Dane axe, Grettir is a terrible threat to any opponent at close range. He wears the traditional bear sark, fashioned from one Frettir's previous adversaries, and takes his cues from Loki, the Norse god of trickery and deceit. As in life, Grettir is one of the most dangerous warriors in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Sterk: Your attacks with axes deal +1 damage. Your attack rolls that are doubles do not count as critical hits.

cards.name: Loki

abilities.name: Kned
abilities.text: Choose an opposing warrior's face-up weapon. If you have a face-up weapon, exchange that card for your opponent's weapon until the end of the round.

cards.flavor: God of trickery and mischief, Loki plagues the Aesir with his crafty, often malicious pranks.
cards.raw_source_text: Kned - Reveal: Choose an opposing warrior's face-up weapon. If you have a face-up weapon, exchange that card for your opponent's weapon until the end of the round.

cards.name: Dane Axe

abilities.name: Hogge
abilities.text: This weapon deals +1 damage for each of your face-up support cards.

cards.flavor: The six-foot broadax can shear the head from a horse in a single blow.
cards.raw_source_text: Hogge: This weapon deals +1 damage for each of your face-up support cards.

cards.name: Bear Sark

abilities.name: Bjorn
abilities.text: Once per round, when you are attacked, force the attacker to re-roll their attack roll. Discard this card if the result is lower than their original roll.

cards.flavor: Wearing this pelt gives the Norse warrior the strength, stamina and power of the bear itself.
cards.raw_source_text: Bjorn: Once per round, when you are attacked, force the attacker to re-roll their attack roll. Discard this card if the result is lower than their original roll.

cards.name: Berserkergangr

abilities.name: Vrede
abilities.text: Your attacks deal +1 damage. You take 1 damage after each of your attacks.

cards.flavor: In fits of rage, beserkers and ulfheonars tear through enemy lines with inhuman strength and ferocity.
cards.raw_source_text: Vrede: Your attacks deal +1 damage. You take 1 damage after each of your attacks.

cards.name: Julius Caesar

abilities.name: Alea Iacta Est
abilities.text: Once per turn, before you roll the dice, the roll is considered to be a 7.

cards.flavor: As a priest, lawyer, warrior, general and Caesar, Gaius Julius leaves his mark both with his mind and with his sword.
cards.background:
A general, politician, lawyer, priest and emperor, Julius Caesar fought on practically every kind of battlefield and emerged victorious. He is famous for his military campaign against the Guals, which he used to bolster his political standing and to lay claim to the title of Caesar.

Inspired by Jupiter, supreme god of the Roman pantheon, Julius Caesar enters the Anachronism arena as one of the greatest generals and statesmen of all time. He carries his most famous writings, "De Bello Gallico" as testament to military success and reference on how to win in any situation. Wielding the gladius - "The sword that conquered the world" - Julius Caesar stands ready to conquer any and all who oppose him.
cards.raw_source_text: Alea Iacta Est: Once per turn, before you roll the dice, the roll is considered to be a 7.

cards.name: Jupiter

abilities.name: Vis Iovis
abilities.text: Each round, if you win initiative, your attacks deal +1 damage that round.

cards.flavor: As supreme god of the Roman pantheon, Jupiter rules the gods, the skies and the weather.
cards.raw_source_text: Vis Iovis: Each round, if you win initiative, your attacks deal +1 damage that round.

cards.name: Gladius

abilities.name: Lamina Acuta
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if you have a face-up shield card. Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if the defender has no face-up armor card.

cards.flavor: Known as "the sword that conquered the world", the gladius is a remarkably balanced thrusting sword.
cards.raw_source_text: Lamina Acuta: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if you have a face-up shield card. Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if the defender has no face-up armor card.

cards.name: Skjold

abilities.name: Ward
abilities.text: Once this round, before an opposing warrior attacks you, you may reduce all of their attack bonuses to zero.

cards.flavor: These flat, round, single-layered shields line the gunwales of long ships and accompany Norse warriors into battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Ward - Reveal: Once this round, before an opposing warrior attacks you, you may reduce all of their attack bonuses to zero.

cards.name: Sverd

abilities.name: Decisive Blow
abilities.text: Once per turn, after one of your attacks with this weapon is a critical hit, you may attack again with this weapon without spending an additional action.

cards.flavor: Forged twisted rods of hard and soft iron make this sword flexible and highly durable.
cards.raw_source_text: Decisive Blow: Once per turn, after one of your attacks with this weapon is a critical hit, you may attack again with this weapon without spending an additional action.

cards.name: Caesar's Greaves

abilities.name: Tutela Crusibus
abilities.text: After you roll doubles on a defense roll, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: Shin guards protect warriors, especially mounted troops, from attacks against their legs.
cards.raw_source_text: Tutela Crusibus: After you roll doubles on a defense roll, gain 1 life.

cards.name: De Bello Gallico

abilities.name: Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 while the total initiative of all your cards is greater than the total initiative of any other warrior's cards.

cards.flavor: Caesar's scrolls outlines his success and experience during the nine-year long Gallic campaigns.
cards.raw_source_text: Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres: Your attack rolls gain +1 while the total initiative of all your cards is greater than the total initiative of any other warrior's cards.

cards.name: Amazonia

abilities.name: Utriusque Manus Compos
abilities.text: You may use up to two weapons.

cards.flavor: Amazonia is the most famous gladiatrix of her time.
cards.background:
While many of the greatest warriors of the past have been men, one of the first recorded women to capture a piece of history with her capability and skill in battle was a gladiatrix named Amazonia. A female gladiator without peer, she fought for her life in the Roman Coliseum.

Amazonia comes complete with the pugio and scutulum - traditional and effective arms for a gladiator of her time. With Mercury, the god of speed, as her inspiration, Amazonia rules the Anachronism arena with her speed and deft use of weaponry.
cards.raw_source_text: Utriusque Manus Compos: You may use up to two weapons.

cards.name: Mercury

abilities.name: Nuntius
abilities.text: All warriors gain +2 speed this round.

cards.flavor: Running on winged heels, Mercury, the god of speed, delivers messages for the immortals.
cards.raw_source_text: Nuntius - Reveal: All warriors gain +2 speed this round.

cards.name: Pugio

abilities.name: Turbare
abilities.text: Attack with this weapon.

cards.flavor: For the Roman Republican or Empirical warrior, the pugio is a favorite tool and backup weapon.
cards.raw_source_text: Turbare - Action: Attack with this weapon.

cards.name: Pteryges

abilities.name: Servata Tergi
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 if the attacker is in any of your attack grids.

cards.flavor: These hanging leather straps protect the upper legs and thighs.
cards.raw_source_text: Servata Tergi: Your defense rolls gain +1 if the attacker is in any of your attack grids.

cards.name: Scutulum

abilities.name: Clipeus
abilities.text: Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 while you have another face-up support card that adds a bonus to your defense rolls.

cards.flavor: Gladiators and Roman auxiliary troops wield the scutulum as a superior melee shield to the scutum.
cards.raw_source_text: Clipeus: Your attack and defense rolls gain +1 while you have another face-up support card that adds a bonus to your defense rolls.

cards.name: Marcus Claudius Marcellus

abilities.name: Gladius Romae
abilities.text: If you are in another warrior's attack grid, make a basic attack on that warrior. You get +1 to your attack roll and this attack deals no more than 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Known as "The Sword of Rome", Marcellus conquers Syracuse, as well as securing innumerable other military victories for the Republic.
cards.background:
Marcus Claudius Marcellus - "The Sword of Rome" - is regarded as one of the greatest generals of the Roman Republic. While in his fifties, this experienced warrior defeated a giant Gual chieftain in single combat saving countless lives on both sides. He is best known for his greatest victory in the capture and sack of Syracuse.

Marcus Claudius Marcellus stands undaunted by any warrior who enters the Anacronism Arena. The sun god Apollo inspires Marcellus to victory, while the sturdy loricae segmenta protects him from physical danger. The "Sword of Rome" now finds himself pitted against some of the greatest combatants of all time. Unfortunately for them, Marcus knows only victory.
cards.raw_source_text: Gladius Romae - Action: If you are in another warrior's attack grid, make a basic attack on that warrior. You get +1 to your attack roll and this attack deals no more than 1 damage.

cards.name: Apollo

abilities.name: Cura Dei
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 this round.

cards.flavor: God of art, light and healing, Apollo pulls the sun across the sky behind his four horse chariot.
cards.raw_source_text: Cura Dei - Reveal: Your attack rolls gain +1 this round.

cards.name: Pilum

abilities.name: Perutilis
abilities.text: Discard this card to deal 1 damage to another warrior and discard that warrior's face-up shield card, if any.

cards.flavor: This metal-tipped javelin bends on impact, disabling shields and making it impossible to throw back.
cards.raw_source_text: Perutilis - Action: Discard this card to deal 1 damage to another warrior and discard that warrior's face-up shield card, if any.

cards.name: Loricae Segmentata

abilities.name: Mobilis
abilities.text: When you take more than 2 damage, reduce that damage to 2.

cards.flavor: This segmented armor, lightweight and flexible, revolutionizes chest protection.
cards.raw_source_text: Mobilis: When you take more than 2 damage, reduce that damage to 2.

cards.name: Disciplina

abilities.name: Si vis pacem, para bellum
abilities.text: After you make an attack roll, you may increase the value of one of the dice by 1 if you won initiative this turn. If the dice are now doubles, the attack may result in a critical hit.

cards.flavor: The exceptional discipline of the Roman troops ensures their success across hundreds of battlefields.
cards.raw_source_text: Si vis pacem, para bellum: After you make an attack roll, you may increase the value of one of the dice by 1 if you won initiative this turn. If the dice are now doubles, the attack may result in a critical hit.

cards.name: Maximinus

abilities.name: Ardor Deorum
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 while you have an Inspiration in play.

cards.flavor: A giant of a man at over 2.6 meters tall, Maximinus is as unforgiving of his subjects as he is of his enemies.
cards.background:
The most physically powerful and intimidating Emperor of ancient Rome, Maximinus ruled through strength of arms. Having risen from the ranks of a soldier, he believed completely in military discipline as the core of successful command both on the battlefield and in politics.

Maximinus sends the enemies of Rome to meet Pluto, god of the underworld. Armed with the spatha, the favored slashing sword of the Roman cavalry, and their barbarian auxiliary, Maximinus fearlessly strides into battle. Protected by the scutum shield and the cassis helmet, Maximinus stands as a colossus in the Anachronism arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Ardor Deorum: Your attack rolls gain +1 while you have an Inspiration in play.

cards.name: Pluto

abilities.name: Mors Venit
abilities.text: At the end of each player's turn, that player's warrior takes 1 damage if they did not hit another warrior that turn.

cards.flavor: Lord of the underworld, Pluto rules over the souls of those fallen in battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Mors Venit: At the end of each player's turn, that player's warrior takes 1 damage if they did not hit another warrior that turn.

cards.name: Spatha

abilities.name: Caedo
abilities.text: After you hit a warrior with this weapon, your basic attacks against that warrior deal +1 damage this round.

cards.flavor: The spatha is the favorite slashing sword of the Roman cavalry and barbarian auxiliary.
cards.raw_source_text: Caedo: After you hit a warrior with this weapon, your basic attacks against that warrior deal +1 damage this round.

cards.name: Cassis

abilities.name: Galeatus
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 if you are a metal or water warrior. You gain +1 experience if you are an earth or fire warrior.

cards.flavor: Roman smiths crafted the cassis to be the most advanced, comfortable and functional helmet in the Roman Empire.
cards.raw_source_text: Galeatus: Your defense rolls gain +1 if you are a metal or water warrior. You gain +1 experience if you are an earth or fire warrior.

cards.name: Scutum

abilities.name: Scutatus
abilities.text: Once per round, after you make a successful attack, roll two dice. If that total is less than or equal to your experience, move the defending warrior one space.

cards.flavor: This convex, reinforced shield protects the legionary and doubles as a ram with its metal shield boss.
cards.raw_source_text: Scutatus: Once per round, after you make a successful attack, roll two dice. If that total is less than or equal to your experience, move the defending warrior one space.

cards.name: Achilles

abilities.name: Iroiki Taxitita
abilities.text: Move one space diagonally.

cards.flavor: Achilles stands as the greatest and most feared warrior of the Trojan War.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Iroiki Taxitita - Action: Move one space diagonally.

cards.name: Thetis

abilities.name: Mitriki Agapi
abilities.text: Once per game, re-roll any one die or make an opponent re-roll any one die.

cards.flavor: Daughter of Nereus and Doris, Thetis is the queen of the sea nymphs and mother in Achilles.
cards.raw_source_text: Mitriki Agapi: Once per game, re-roll any one die or make an opponent re-roll any one die.

cards.name: Sarissa

abilities.name: Katastrofi panoplias
abilities.text: Opponent's armor cards produce no effects during the first round they are revealed.

cards.flavor: This broad-leaf, 2.5-meter spear strikes fear into the hearts of those who oppose the Greek hoplite.
cards.raw_source_text: Katastrofi panoplias: Opponent's armor cards produce no effects during the first round they are revealed.

cards.name: Armor of Hephaestus

abilities.name: Eulogimeni Panoplia
abilities.text: Once per round, when you attack, you may make your enemy re-roll their defense roll. Discard this card if the result is lower than their original roll.

cards.flavor: After Hector takes Achilles' armor, Hephaestus forges a replacement panoply like nothing ever seen.
cards.raw_source_text: Eulogimeni Panoplia: Once per round, when you attack, you may make your enemy re-roll their defense roll. Discard this card if the result is lower than their original roll.

cards.name: Shield of Hephaestus

abilities.name: Dia Tis Pistis Aminomenos
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have a face-up inspiration card.

cards.flavor: After Achilles' death, Ajax and Odyssus fight for possession of this five-layered shield.
cards.raw_source_text: Dia Tis Pistis Aminomenos: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have a face-up inspiration card.

cards.name: Spartacus

abilities.name: Ars Gladiatoria
abilities.text: If you have an inspiration in play, make an extra attack with one of your weapons.

cards.flavor: Thracian gladiator, slave and rebel leader, Spartacus emancipates himself and wreaks havoc on the Roman Empire.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Ars Gladiatoria - 2 Actions: If you have an inspiration in play, make an extra attack with one of your weapons.

cards.name: Blossius of Cumae

abilities.name: Primus ultimus erit, atque ultimus primus
abilities.text: If you are a metal warrior, resolve initiative from lowest to highest this challenge.

cards.flavor: Stoic philosopher Blossius of Cumae stirs the soul to action, regardless of the Empire and its laws.
cards.raw_source_text: Primus ultimus erit, atque ultimus primus: If you are a metal warrior, resolve initiative from lowest to highest this challenge.

cards.name: Sica

abilities.name: Lamina Incurvata
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if the defending warrior has a face-up shield card.

cards.flavor: Thracian gladiators use this curved, short sword to inflict damage around their opponents' shields.
cards.raw_source_text: Lamina Incurvata: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage if the defending warrior has a face-up shield card.

cards.name: Ocrea

abilities.name: Armatura
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 if you are a metal warrior.

cards.flavor: This combination of shin and thigh guards protects the gladiator's legs and ensures vital and continued mobility.
cards.raw_source_text: Armatura: Your defense rolls gain +1 if you are a metal warrior.

cards.name: Parma

abilities.name: In Vicem Oppugnare
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 while you are not in an opponent's basic attack grid.

cards.flavor: Lightweight, small, round or square shields are well suited for arena and melee combat.
cards.raw_source_text: In Vicem Oppugnare: Your attack rolls gain +1 while you are not in an opponent's basic attack grid.
